scripts.name: Manage Vault
scripts.description: Full vault management — create, edit, and organise vault entries
scripts.help: CAWC Vault Session Manager. Handles course registration and pre-session setup. Launches a full interactive TUI — use its own menus to navigate. Returns here when you exit.

scripts.name: Vault Health
scripts.description: Check vault integrity and surface broken or orphaned entries
scripts.help: Scans the CAWC Obsidian vault for broken wikilinks and orphaned notes. Ignores index files (prefixed _), image links, and stub notes. Outputs a report to the terminal and writes a markdown report to Obsidian at Help/Vault Health Report.md.

scripts.name: Lecture Merge
scripts.description: Merge Panopto lecture recordings into a single clean video
scripts.help: Auto-classifies 3 Panopto .mp4 files by audio and bitrate, normalizes audio to EBU R128 (-16 LUFS), optionally overlays screen recording as PiP, and outputs a named session file. Place 3 .mp4 files in the Video Staging folder before running.

scripts.name: PDF → Text
scripts.description: Extract plain text from one or more PDF files
scripts.help: Converts PDFs to plain text using bundled pymupdf. Layout mode (default) preserves columns and tables; plain mode extracts pure reading-order text. No external dependencies.

scripts.name: Images → PDF
scripts.description: Combine images into a single PDF
scripts.help: Combines PNG, JPG, JPEG, TIFF, or BMP images into a single PDF in the order queued. Add files individually or pick a folder to include all images in it (sorted alphabetically). Output goes to the folder of the first image.

scripts.name: PPTX → PDF
scripts.help: Converts .pptx files to PDF using Microsoft PowerPoint. Queue files individually or pick a folder. Compression (default: yes) optimizes file size via pymupdf — toggle off to keep PowerPoint's raw output. Requires Microsoft PowerPoint installed. macOS only for now.

scripts.name: PPTX → Text
scripts.description: Extract plain text from PowerPoint presentations
scripts.help: Converts .pptx files to plain text in one step. Internally runs PowerPoint export to PDF, then text extraction with layout reconstruction. The intermediate PDF is kept by default. Requires Microsoft PowerPoint installed. macOS only for now.

argDefs.label: Image quality
argDefs.tooltip: DPI for embedded images in the intermediate PDF. medium = 150 DPI (balanced). small = 72 DPI (smallest). large = 300 DPI (print quality). none = PowerPoint raw.

argDefs.label: Text layout
argDefs.tooltip: layout preserves columns and tables via row reconstruction. plain extracts continuous reading-order text.

argDefs.label: Output folder
argDefs.tooltip: Where to save the final .txt file. Intermediate PDF stays beside the input.

argDefs.label: Keep intermediate PDF
argDefs.checkboxLabel: Keep the PDF produced during conversion
argDefs.tooltip: Checked (default) keeps the intermediate .pdf alongside the .txt. Unchecked discards it after the .txt is produced.

scripts.name: Widget: Checkbox
scripts.description: Test the checkbox widget end-to-end via --echo
scripts.help: Verify the checkbox widget. The script runs in --echo mode and prints the argv it received. Toggle the checkbox and run; check whether --no-keep-intermediate appears in received_argv when unchecked, and is absent when checked.

argDefs.label: Keep intermediate files
argDefs.checkboxLabel: Keep all stage outputs on disk
argDefs.tooltip: When checked, passes nothing. When unchecked, passes --no-keep-intermediate.

scripts.name: Widget: Number
scripts.description: Test the number widget end-to-end via --echo
scripts.help: Verify the number widget. Try values inside and outside the min/max range. Empty input should omit the flag entirely; any value should appear as --pdf-layout <value> in received_argv.

argDefs.label: Test number
argDefs.tooltip: Numbers between 36 and 600. Empty = flag omitted. (Flag name reused for debug; ignore semantics.)

scripts.name: Widget: Output Folder
scripts.description: Test the output-folder widget end-to-end via --echo
scripts.help: Verify the output-folder widget. Click 'Pick Folder' and select any directory; the path should appear next to --out-dir in received_argv. Leave blank to omit the flag.

scripts.name: Widget: All Combined
scripts.description: Exercise every new widget in one entry
scripts.help: Combined exercise. Set values across all widgets and run; received_argv shows exactly what reached the script. Useful for verifying interactions (e.g. checkbox + number + outputDir don't collide).
